mentions.note: Cash-flow story, not earnings story; bountiful cash flow; Kelly Ortberg turning the corner; favorite stock in travel trust, up 14% YTD; hold on through January 27 earnings.
Favorite aerospace play; long-term cash-flow story; Kelly Ortberg turning company around.

Cramer thesis: bought relentlessly on the way down (50%, 60%, 70% declines) because management promised cash flow would be higher than expected; followed the cash flow metric, not headlines; stock made new high today; psychology of market had written off Boeing but fundamentals support recovery.
Had monster move post-accident recovery; don't sell as multi-year turnaround play is early innings. Stock at pre-Alaska Airlines accident levels.
Took charge on tanker contract per Pentagon efficiency demands; stock would be up big without charge but big position for travel trust; management holding line on dividends.
Sam mentioned; Cramer owns for Chapel Trust; large backlog supports aerospace suppliers.
Record backlog despite share decline; Middle East concerns overblown. Big position in trust; right stock, right time.
War narrative wrongly assumes fewer plane orders; backlog stretched far. Stock soaring then sold off on sentiment, not fundamentals. Charming name, but underlying thesis intact.
Cramer says will be one of big stocks of 2026; can run much higher; refreshed and ready. Caller Bill asked on adding—Cramer enthusiastically endorsed. Up 6 at one point but going higher.
Own for travel trust. Defense/aerospace tailwind post-Feb-28. World changed; military spending now attractive. Terrific situation given geopolitical shift.
Advanced air-mobility tech play; positioned well for flying-car type technologies if they develop. Better alternative to pure-play flying-car startups.
Wednesday earnings; war fears that crushed stock now lifted; conference call should address this positively; Charitable Trust holding; storm of prior selling now reversed.
Down 11% from anticipation of big order. Good company but stocks don't move up indefinitely; be careful about chasing after big runs.
Trading with Middle East geopolitical risk; travel concerns overblown but market still pricing in weakness; avoid until geopolitical clarity improves or stock trades materially lower.
